**Constantin Zamfir** (15 May 1951 – 3 November 2025) was a Romanian footballer who played as a [midfielder](/wiki/Midfielder "Midfielder").[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3)

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Constantin_Zamfir&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

Zamfir, nicknamed *Papucul Zburător* (The Flying Shoe), was born on 15 May 1951 in [Bucșani](/wiki/Buc%C8%99ani,_Giurgiu "Bucșani, Giurgiu"), Romania.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) In 1969, he began playing football at [Divizia C](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III") club Petrolul Videle.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) Afterwards he went to play for Voința Ilia and [Dacia Orăștie](/wiki/CSM_Dacia_Or%C4%83%C8%99tie "CSM Dacia Orăștie"), before returning to Videle in 1972.[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) Subsequently, in 1973, he joined [Petrolul Ploiești](/wiki/FC_Petrolul_Ploie%C8%99ti "FC Petrolul Ploiești") where he made his [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") debut on 12 August 1973 under coach Gheorghe Dumitrescu in a 2–1 away loss to [CFR Cluj](/wiki/CFR_Cluj "CFR Cluj").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[4]](#cite_note-4) The team was relegated at the end of his [first season](/wiki/1973%E2%80%9374_Divizia_A "1973–74 Divizia A"), but Zamfir stayed with the club for one more year.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)

In 1975, he joined [Steaua București](/wiki/FC_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Steaua București") which sent eight players to Petrolul in exchange for his transfer, including [Florian Dumitrescu](/wiki/Florian_Dumitrescu "Florian Dumitrescu") and [Nicolae Pantea](/wiki/Nicolae_Pantea "Nicolae Pantea").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) In the summer of that year, the club participated in a tournament in [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain") where they played against [Sevilla](/wiki/Sevilla_FC "Sevilla FC"), [Espanyol Barcelona](/wiki/RCD_Espanyol "RCD Espanyol"), [Cádiz](/wiki/C%C3%A1diz_CF "Cádiz CF") and [Granada](/wiki/Granada_CF "Granada CF").[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) Zamfir made a good impression and was nicknamed by the Spanish press as "The New [Gento](/wiki/Paco_Gento "Paco Gento")".[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) Sevilla also made an offer of 2 million [pesetas](/wiki/Spanish_peseta "Spanish peseta") for him, but he was denied the transfer.[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) On 2 November 1975, he netted a goal in a 3–3 draw against [rivals](/wiki/Eternal_derby_(Romania) "Eternal derby (Romania)") [Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București").[[5]](#cite_note-5) He finished his [first season](/wiki/1975%E2%80%9376_Divizia_A "1975–76 Divizia A") at Steaua by winning [The Double](/wiki/Double_(association_football) "Double (association football)"), being used by coach [Emerich Jenei](/wiki/Emerich_Jenei "Emerich Jenei") in 32 league games in which he scored eight goals, and played the entire match in the 2–1 win over [CSU Galați](/wiki/CSU_Dun%C4%83rea_de_Jos_Gala%C8%9Bi "CSU Dunărea de Jos Galați") in the [Cupa României final](/wiki/1976_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei_final "1976 Cupa României final").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3)[[6]](#cite_note-RSC-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) He reached another [Cupa României final](/wiki/1977_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei_final "1977 Cupa României final") in 1977, where Jenei used him the full 90 minutes in the eventual 2–1 loss to [Universitatea Craiova](/wiki/CS_Universitatea_Craiova "CS Universitatea Craiova").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[8]](#cite_note-RSCR-8) In the [1977–78](/wiki/1977%E2%80%9378_Divizia_A "1977–78 Divizia A") season, Zamfir scored three goals in the 24 league games Jenei used him, helping the club win another title.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3)[[6]](#cite_note-RSC-6) The team reached the [1979 Cupa României final](/wiki/1979_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei_final "1979 Cupa României final") under coach [Gheorghe Constantin](/wiki/Gheorghe_Constantin "Gheorghe Constantin"), Zamfir playing the entire match in the 3–0 win over [Sportul Studențesc București](/wiki/FC_Sportul_Studen%C8%9Besc_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Sportul Studențesc București").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3)[[9]](#cite_note-9) In his five-season spell with *[The Military Men](/wiki/FC_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Steaua București")* he also played eight matches in European competitions, most notably getting past [Young Boys](/wiki/BSC_Young_Boys "BSC Young Boys") with an 8–2 aggregate win in the first round of the [1979–80 European Cup Winners' Cup](/wiki/1979%E2%80%9380_European_Cup_Winners%27_Cup "1979–80 European Cup Winners' Cup").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In the middle of the [1979–80](/wiki/1979%E2%80%9380_Divizia_A "1979–80 Divizia A") season, Zamfir left Steaua to join [Divizia B](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II") club [Progresul București](/wiki/AS_Progresul_Bucure%C8%99ti "AS Progresul București"), helping them earn promotion to Divizia A at the end of the season.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) For the [following season](/wiki/1980%E2%80%9381_Divizia_A "1980–81 Divizia A") he went to play in the first league for [FCM Galați](/wiki/FCM_Dun%C4%83rea_Gala%C8%9Bi "FCM Dunărea Galați") which was relegated at the end of the season.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3)[[11]](#cite_note-11) Subsequently, he returned to Progresul for the [1981–82 Divizia A](/wiki/1981%E2%80%9382_Divizia_A "1981–82 Divizia A") season, but suffered another relegation at the end of it.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) He made his last Divizia A appearance on 9 June 1982 in Progresul's 3–1 home loss to [Olt Scornicești](/wiki/FC_Olt_Scornice%C8%99ti "FC Olt Scornicești"), totaling 157 matches with 30 goals in the competition.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[12]](#cite_note-12) Zamfir ended his career after playing several years in the Romanian lower leagues for teams such as [Metalurgistul Cugir](/wiki/CF_Metalurgistul_Cugir "CF Metalurgistul Cugir"), [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești"), [Chimia Turnu Măgurele](/wiki/CS_Dun%C4%83rea_Turris_Turnu_M%C4%83gurele "CS Dunărea Turris Turnu Măgurele"), Urbis București, Laromet București, [Inter Vaslui](/wiki/Inter_Vaslui "Inter Vaslui") and Petrolul Roata de Jos.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) At the latter, he worked as a player-coach.[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Constantin_Zamfir&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International career")]

Between 1975 and 1979, Zamfir made several appearances for [Romania's under-21](/wiki/Romania_national_under-21_football_team "Romania national under-21 football team"), under-23, B and [Olympic](/wiki/Romania_Olympic_football_team "Romania Olympic football team") teams.[[13]](#cite_note-v-13) Notably, he scored a goal for the Olympic side in a 5–1 victory against the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands_national_football_team "Netherlands national football team") during the [1976 Summer Olympics qualifiers](/wiki/Football_at_the_1976_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_qualification "Football at the 1976 Summer Olympics – Men's qualification").[[13]](#cite_note-v-13)

Zamfir played 11 games for [Romania](/wiki/Romania_national_football_team "Romania national football team"), making his debut on 16 November 1975 under coach [Valentin Stănescu](/wiki/Valentin_St%C4%83nescu "Valentin Stănescu") in a 2–2 draw against [Spain](/wiki/Spain_national_football_team "Spain national football team") in the [Euro 1976 qualifiers](/wiki/UEFA_Euro_1976_qualifying_Group_4 "UEFA Euro 1976 qualifying Group 4").[[14]](#cite_note-EU-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) Afterwards he made three appearances during the [1978 World Cup qualifiers](/wiki/1978_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification "1978 FIFA World Cup qualification"), including two victories against Spain and [Yugoslavia](/wiki/Yugoslavia_national_football_team "Yugoslavia national football team").[[14]](#cite_note-EU-14) He also played three matches during the [1977–80 Balkan Cup](/wiki/1977%E2%80%9380_Balkan_Cup "1977–80 Balkan Cup"), including his last match for the national team which took place on 31 May 1978 in a 1–1 draw against [Bulgaria](/wiki/Bulgaria_national_football_team "Bulgaria national football team").[[14]](#cite_note-EU-14)

## Coaching career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Constantin_Zamfir&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Coaching career")]

Zamfir was a player-coach at Petrolul Roata de Jos in the Romanian lower leagues.[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3) Afterwards he coached juniors for clubs such as [Concordia Chiajna](/wiki/CS_Concordia_Chiajna "CS Concordia Chiajna").[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Style of play

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Constantin_Zamfir&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Style of play")]

[Emerich Jenei](/wiki/Emerich_Jenei "Emerich Jenei"), his coach from [Steaua București](/wiki/FCSB "FCSB"), said about him: "I have coached few footballers in my career who had as many qualities as Zamfir."[[3]](#cite_note-prosport-3)[[17]](#cite_note-GSP-17) Zamfir was one of [Ilie Dumitrescu](/wiki/Ilie_Dumitrescu "Ilie Dumitrescu")'s childhood idols, who described his style of play as: "He had very good speed, he was explosive, and in one-on-one duels he was very good. He excelled at speed dribbling. (...) He was a great talent. He was a classic winger. The style that [Giggs](/wiki/Ryan_Giggs "Ryan Giggs") had. He had speed, he crossed very well."[[17]](#cite_note-GSP-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Constantin_Zamfir&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Death")]

Zamfir died on 3 November 2025, at the age of 74.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Constantin_Zamfir&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Honours")]

**Steaua București**

* [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"): [1975–76](/wiki/1975%E2%80%9376_Divizia_A "1975–76 Divizia A"), [1977–78](/wiki/1977%E2%80%9378_Divizia_A "1977–78 Divizia A")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)
* [Cupa României](/wiki/Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "Cupa României"): [1975–76](/wiki/1975%E2%80%9376_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1975–76 Cupa României"), [1978–79](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1978–79 Cupa României"), runner-up [1976–77](/wiki/1976%E2%80%9377_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1976–77 Cupa României")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[8]](#cite_note-RSCR-8)

**Progresul București**

* [Divizia B](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II"): [1979–80](/wiki/1979%E2%80%9380_Divizia_B "1979–80 Divizia B")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)
